title: Installation - SonarLint Documentation - Eclipse
content:
# Installation

## [Instructions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/getting-started/installation/#instructions "Instructions")

SonarQube for Eclipse is a plugin that can be installed in most Eclipse-based IDEs (including Spring Tool Suite, PyDev, and others). You can find the SonarLint for Eclipse IDE extension directly on the [Eclipse Marketplace](https://marketplace.eclipse.org/content/sonarlint "Eclipse Marketplace").

### [Using the Eclipse Marketplace client](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/getting-started/installation/#using-the-eclipse-marketplace-client "Using the Eclipse Marketplace client")

1\. In your Eclipse-based IDE, open the Marketplace client.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Go to Help > Eclipse Marketplace... to install SonarLint directly from the IDE.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/664341ea-c61d-49f5-b661-0147f4ffdc35/eclipse-open-marketplace-client.png?w=447&h=409&auto=format&fit=crop)

2\. Search for `sonarqube`, and select **Install***.*

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Install SonarLint for Eclipse directly from the Eclipse Marketplace.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/43e1dee0-9285-4561-acb7-36ab764cde03/eclipse-install-from-marketplace.png?w=1096&h=636&auto=format&fit=crop)

3\. Restart your IDE.

### [Using the Eclipse Install New Software wizard](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/getting-started/installation/#using-the-eclipseinstall-new-softwarewizard "Using the Eclipse Install New Software wizard")

If you can't use the Eclipse Marketplace client, you can still directly access the SonarQube for Eclipse update site. See the [official Eclipse documentation](https://help.eclipse.org/?topic=%2Forg.eclipse.platform.doc.user%2Ftasks%2Ftasks-124.htm "official Eclipse documentation") for full details, and follow these instructions:

1. Go to **Eclipse** > **Help** and select **Install New Software**.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Go to Help > Install New Software... in the Eclipse file menu to start your SonarLint installation.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/321d0768-702b-4c15-9da8-4117c224b6a4/eclipse-install-new-software-wizard.png?w=424&h=396&auto=format&fit=crop)

2. Select the **Add...** button to add a new repository pointing to [https://eclipse-uc.sonarlint.org](https://eclipse-uc.sonarlint.org/ "https://eclipse-uc.sonarlint.org"). Give the repository a meaningful name. Don't worry if you, can't open this URL in your web browser, Eclipse will automatically look for files [compositeContent.xml](https://eclipse-uc.sonarlint.org/compositeContent.xml "compositeContent.xml") and [compositeArtifacts.xml](https://eclipse-uc.sonarlint.org/compositeArtifacts.xml "compositeArtifacts.xml").

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Select Add to enter the SonarLint for Eclipse repository URL.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/46486c32-8295-49ec-b103-cecdd8828279/eclipse-add-software-site.png?w=1068&h=354&auto=format&fit=crop)

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Add the SonarLint repository URL to the Location field in the Installation Wizard.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/0c618331-0f19-4255-92f6-87fcc0876a9b/eclipse-add-software-site-online.png?w=1068&h=422&auto=format&fit=crop)

3. Select both the newly added *SonarQube for Eclipse repository* and the **SonarLint for Eclipse** feature.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Select the SonarLint dependencies and packages to fit your application.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/cbaef657-f381-40e3-a530-d9a8b7367fae/eclipse-add-sonarlint-repository.png?w=1068&h=731&auto=format&fit=crop)

4\. Review the features about to be installed, and select **Finish**.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![The last step before finishing your SonarLint for Eclipse installation!](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/9c5c651d-3b77-4c9b-a363-3b25d5568bf9/eclipse-add-sonarlint-repository-confirm.png?w=1068&h=485&auto=format&fit=crop)

5\. When requested, restart your IDE

By using this method, you will be automatically notified of new updates of SonarQube for Eclipse (assuming you have allowed Eclipse to check for updates).

## [First taste of SonarQube](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/getting-started/installation/#first-taste-of-sonarlint "First taste of SonarQube")

Now that you have SonarQube for Eclipse installed, open or create a new project containing source files in a programming language that SonarQube for Eclipse can analyze out of the box: Java, PHP, Python, JavaScripts or HTML. See the [Rules and languages](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/using/rules/ "Rules and languages") page for a full list of available languages.

For example in Java, you can copy-paste this code snippet, with a typical bug when copy-pasting `for` loops:

`package org.mycompany;  import java.util.logging.Level; import java.util.logging.Logger;  public class Main {    private static final Logger LOGGER = Logger.getLogger(Main.class.getName());    public static void main(String[] args) {     for (int left = 0; left < 10; left++) {       for (int right = 0; right < 10; left++) {         LOGGER.log(Level.INFO, "Pair: ({0},{1})", new Object[] {left, right});       }     }   }  }`

If you open this Java file within the Eclipse Java editor, you should see SonarQube for Eclipse reporting the issue:

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![SonarLint gives you squiggly lines and a ruled line in the sidebar to help call out issues in the Eclipse code explorer.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/464d3077-b755-4ea7-87c6-115a3501f8c9/eclipse-run-first-analysis.png?w=1429&h=845&auto=format&fit=crop)

To get more details about the issue, you can simply hover on the issue location, and a popup will display the issue message:

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Hover over an issue found by SonarLint to pull up the issue message and quick fix (when available).](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/d28afbd2-a4b5-443a-89c3-d0fcac19ee01/eclipse-hover-on-java-issue.png?w=660&h=173&auto=format&fit=crop)

There is also the possibility to use the SonarQube for Eclipse **On-The-Fly** feature to display the list of issues found by SonarQube for Eclipse. Simply open the view from the menu **Windows** \> **Show View** \> **Other**.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![To open a SonarLint view that isn't showing, go to Windows > Show View > Other > SonarLint and choose your views.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/3605abfa-0116-420a-994e-9af546b43b91/eclipse-open-on-the-fly-view.png?w=369&h=452&auto=format&fit=crop)

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![The SonarLint On-The-Fly view shows you issues found in the active file.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/fde6ea6e-9d88-4999-b8d6-cc3f588288b2/eclipse-view-issue-on-the-fly.png?w=1056&h=247&auto=format&fit=crop)
